title: dalechampia tiliifolia var. peruviana
intro: Dalechampia tiliifolia var. peruviana, also known as the Peruvian rainforest touch-me-not or Peruvian horseseed, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dalechampia tiliifolia var. peruviana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
